definitions: A type of hydraulic fluid used in hydraulic brakes and hydraulic clutches within automotive, motorcycle, and some bicycle applications.
A non-compressible fluid that transmits force from the master cylinder to the brake calipers or wheel cylinders, causing the brake pads or shoes to engage.
examples: Check the brake fluid level regularly.
Low brake fluid can indicate a leak.
DOT 3 and DOT 4 are common types of brake fluid.
